title: henckelia salicina
intro: Henckelia salicina, also known as the salicine-tailed Henckelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Western Ghats of India, where it grows in the wild. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Henckelia salicina include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions.
